---
title: "timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_aa"
subtitle: "ribostall stall_sites — enrichment stats"
date: 2026-05-28
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [7, 23, 16, 16, 20, 18]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![2x3 grid of day-10-vs-day-0 amino-acid Fisher volcano panels: rows are conditions (top = BWM, bottom = control); columns are ribosome sites (A / P / E). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) against log2(odds ratio); triangles mark FDR<0.05 hits and circles are non-significant. Glyphs are coloured by amino-acid property (acidic = red, basic = blue, hydrophobic = green, neutral = orange, polar = purple). Horizontal dashed line is FDR = 0.05; vertical dashed lines mark +/-0.5 log2(OR).](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/composite/aa_fisher_composite.png)

Each glyph is a single amino acid at one (condition, site) BH-FDR family of 20. Positive log2(OR) means day-10-enriched relative to day-0 *within the same condition*; negative means day-10-depleted relative to day-0. The test does not compare BWM to control directly — that contrast is implied by reading paired top/bottom rows of the grid side-by-side.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_aa.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `timepoint_fisher_within_condition`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d0_codon.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_aa.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5_codon.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_aa.csv`
  - `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d5_vs_d0_codon.csv`
- **Test:** Fisher's exact (two-sided), day-10-vs-day-0 2x2 contingency per (condition, site, aa); BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 AAs
- **Design:** Per (condition, site, aa) 2x2 day-10-vs-day-0 contingency tested by two-sided Fisher's exact within one condition; replicates summed before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR applied independently within each of 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs (120 tests total). The test is **within-condition**: BWM and control are tested separately and never share a contingency.

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — 2 biological reps per (condition, timepoint) are summed into the 2x2 before Fisher's exact; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative** — pooled (day_10 + day_0) totals at the condition level are in the thousands; Fisher returns vanishing p-values for tiny absolute deviations, so `log2_OR` is the primary effect column, not p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(condition,site)** — BH-FDR is computed independently within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs; `p_adj` means "corrected within this sub-family", not across the full 120-row file.
- **within-condition-clean** — unlike the between-condition or between-timepoint Wilcoxon families, no condition pooling and no timepoint pooling cross-contaminate the test; the design is structurally cleaner.

---

## Headline

Strong signal at AA-level within-condition Fisher d10 vs d0: 36/120 hits at FDR<0.05 (12/60 BWM, 24/60 control). BWM is dominated by site-A direction shifts (N enriched `log2_OR`=+0.83; L, Y, A depleted) plus site-E K depleted with Q/S/C enriched and site-P K depleted / E enriched. Control is dominated by broad site-P shifts (G, E, R, D, C enriched; M, Q, K, I, L, W depleted) and site-A G/R/K/I enriched vs Q/L/W/S/Y depleted. Three largest-magnitude BWM-vs-control direction-divergent cells: site-A N (BWM enriched `log2_OR`=+0.83 vs control flat `log2_OR`=-0.22, p_adj=0.053 ns), site-E K (BWM depleted `log2_OR`=-0.34 vs control enriched `log2_OR`=+0.18), and site-P G (BWM flat `log2_OR`=+0.01 vs control enriched `log2_OR`=+0.36). Largest-magnitude shared-direction cells: site-P E both enriched (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.40 / control `log2_OR`=+0.22), and site-P K both depleted (BWM `log2_OR`=-0.40 / control `log2_OR`=-0.23).

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_OR` (log2 of the Fisher odds ratio for the day-10-vs-day-0 contrast within one condition; positive = day-10-enriched relative to day-0, negative = day-10-depleted). Each row pairs the BWM and control `log2_OR` for one site x amino-acid cell. The `effect change` column is the BWM `log2_OR` minus the control `log2_OR`: it measures how differently the two conditions shifted from day 0 to day 10. A value near zero means both conditions moved together (shared timecourse drift); a large-magnitude value means the two conditions' day-10-vs-day-0 trajectories diverge -- positive when BWM rose relative to control, negative when control rose relative to BWM. The three sections split every cell significant at FDR<0.05 in at least one condition by which condition(s) reach significance: both, BWM only, control only. Within each section rows are grouped by `site` in canonical A / P / E order, then sorted by `effect change` descending. Cells significant in neither condition are omitted. Each amino-acid one-letter code is expanded to `<letter> (<full name>)` on first appearance in each section.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in both conditions

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | I (isoleucine) | +0.303 | +0.199 | +0.104 |  |
| A | L (leucine) | -0.447 | -0.325 | -0.122 |  |
| A | Y (tyrosine) | -0.451 | -0.209 | -0.242 |  |
| P | E (glutamate) | +0.403 | +0.218 | +0.185 |  |
| P | K (lysine) | -0.402 | -0.230 | -0.172 |  |
| E | K (lysine) | -0.339 | +0.176 | -0.515 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in BWM only

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | N (asparagine) | +0.830 | -0.222 | +1.051 |  |
| A | F (phenylalanine) | +0.379 | +0.064 | +0.315 |  |
| A | A (alanine) | -0.353 | -0.020 | -0.333 |  |
| E | C (cysteine) | +0.838 | +0.011 | +0.827 | low-count (BWM, C) |
| E | Q (glutamine) | +0.512 | -0.075 | +0.587 |  |
| E | S (serine) | +0.377 | +0.019 | +0.358 |  |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### Significant in control only

| site | amino acid | BWM (`log2_OR`) | control (`log2_OR`) | effect change | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | Q (glutamine) | -0.069 | -0.606 | +0.537 |  |
| A | W (tryptophan) | -0.385 | -0.604 | +0.220 | low-count (BWM, C) |
| A | S (serine) | -0.030 | -0.232 | +0.202 |  |
| A | R (arginine) | +0.099 | +0.335 | -0.236 |  |
| A | K (lysine) | -0.119 | +0.193 | -0.312 |  |
| A | G (glycine) | +0.025 | +0.365 | -0.339 |  |
| P | Q (glutamine) | +0.195 | -0.438 | +0.633 |  |
| P | W (tryptophan) | -0.269 | -0.699 | +0.430 | low-count (BWM, C) |
| P | M (methionine) | -0.328 | -0.681 | +0.352 | low-count (C) |
| P | I (isoleucine) | -0.069 | -0.245 | +0.177 |  |
| P | L (leucine) | -0.063 | -0.228 | +0.165 |  |
| P | C (cysteine) | +0.431 | +0.341 | +0.090 | low-count (BWM, C) |
| P | D (aspartate) | +0.112 | +0.159 | -0.047 |  |
| P | G (glycine) | +0.011 | +0.362 | -0.350 |  |
| P | R (arginine) | -0.190 | +0.224 | -0.415 |  |
| E | W (tryptophan) | +0.278 | -0.552 | +0.830 | low-count (BWM, C) |
| E | Y (tyrosine) | +0.136 | -0.450 | +0.586 | low-count (C) |
| E | M (methionine) | -0.103 | -0.395 | +0.291 | low-count (C) |

### Flag glossary

- `low-count` — at least one timepoint cell for the named condition arm has `day_X_count` below the test's stability threshold (typically < 100 in BWM, < 200 in control; e.g. BWM,E,C has day_10=69 / day_0=34); the parenthetical suffix names which arm(s) tripped it, where `BWM` = BWM and `C` = control (so `(BWM, C)` means both arms are low, `(C)` means only control is). `log2_OR` estimates and CIs are unstable at small k; magnitude weight should account for the count rather than rank by `log2_OR` alone. **Threshold asymmetry rationale:** BWM_total ~6k vs control_total ~28k (~4.55:1); the 100/200 split is heuristic, calibrated to catch rare AAs (W, C, M) in both arms — symmetric (100/100) under-flags control's rare cells (which sit at 150-300), proportional (100/450) over-flags. Fisher OR variance is dominated by the smallest cell ($\approx 1/a + 1/b + 1/c + 1/d$), so absolute count — not proportion — is the relevant axis. Codon resolution stays symmetric at 50/50 because the AA-vs-codon dilution narrows the relative cell-size spread.

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 120 (60 per condition)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 36 (BWM 12, control 24)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 40 (BWM 13, control 27)
- `min adjusted-p`: 9.89e-08 (tied: control,A,G and control,A,Q within their family); 5.67e-08 (control,P,G, the file minimum)
- `p_floor`: n/a — Fisher with pooled N in the thousands has no meaningful floor; the dominant statistical-design concern is `large-N-Fisher-anticonservative` (family-wide), not floor.
- Per (condition, site):
  - BWM,A: 6 sig at FDR<0.05 (N, L, Y, A, I, F); min p_adj = 9.17e-07
  - BWM,P: 2 sig (K, E); min p_adj = 5.51e-05
  - BWM,E: 4 sig (K, Q, S, C); min p_adj = 4.52e-05
  - control,A: 9 sig (G, Q, L, R, W, K, I, S, Y); min p_adj = 9.89e-08
  - control,P: 11 sig (G, M, Q, K, E, I, L, R, W, D, C); min p_adj = 5.67e-08
  - control,E: 4 sig (Y, K, M, W); min p_adj = 0.00358

## Biological interpretation

**Day-10 control ribosomes have undergone a broad site-P composition reshuffling that BWM has not (concrete).** Control,P contributes 11/24 of its FDR<0.05 hits — including G enriched (`log2_OR`=+0.36), the E/R/D/C up-set alongside G, and the M/Q/K/I/L/W down-band — and the corresponding control,P volcano panel is the most-populated single panel of the file. The asymmetry is explicit in the Top-hits sections: *significant in control only* holds 18 site x amino-acid cells against just 6 in *significant in BWM only*. The mirror BWM,P panel has only 2 FDR<0.05 hits (E enriched, K depleted). The biology this anchors is more compositional than mechanistic: across a 10-day in-vivo culture, the unperturbed (control) ribosome population is sampling a different spectrum of P-site residues than at day 0, while the BWM-perturbed line is holding steadier at site P. Whether that reflects developmental drift in the wild-type animal across the timecourse, a shift in transcript composition, or an actual change in elongation kinetics is not resolvable from this file alone — but the magnitude of the control-side reshuffling sets a baseline against which the BWM-side numbers should be read.

**Lys at the E site flips direction between the two conditions (concrete).** Site-E K is BWM-depleted at day 10 vs day 0 (`log2_OR`=-0.34) but control-enriched at day 10 vs day 0 (`log2_OR`=+0.18). This is the file's clearest divergent-direction signature in BWM vs control, and the only one of the six *significant in both* cells that changes direction -- its `effect change` of -0.515 is the largest-magnitude divergence in that section: within BWM, day-10 ribosomes are *less* likely to have Lys exiting the E site than at day 0; within control, day-10 ribosomes are *more* likely. Lys is the canonical basic-residue exit-tunnel slowing residue (electrostatic interaction with rRNA backbone), so a divergent E-site Lys trajectory across the timecourse is consistent with the two conditions having different basic-residue traffic through the exit channel by day 10. Worth carrying forward to the codon-resolution sister CSV (does the K direction split localize to AAA or AAG?) and to the d5_vs_d0 contrast (is the d10 flip already present at d5, or does it emerge only by d10?).

**P-site Glu and P-site Lys are the two largest-magnitude shared-direction cells (concrete).** Both BWM,P,E enriched (`log2_OR`=+0.40) and control,P,E enriched (`log2_OR`=+0.22) move the same way: more Glu at the P site at day 10 than day 0 in both conditions. And both BWM,P,K depleted (`log2_OR`=-0.40) and the control,P depletion signature (containing K alongside M/W/Q/I/L) point the same way: less Lys at P at day 10 than day 0 in both conditions. Both cells sit in the *significant in both* section with small `effect change` (P:E +0.185, P:K -0.172), the signature of a shared trajectory rather than a divergence. Shared-direction effects in a within-condition contrast are the design's least confounded reading — they describe how each condition's day_10 stall composition differs from its own day_0 baseline in a way that the two conditions agree on, so condition-specific signals (e.g. the BWM perturbation) are not what's driving these particular rows. Most parsimonious mechanistic anchor: across the 10-day window in this organism, ribosomes are sampling a slightly different P-site residue distribution (Glu in, Lys out) regardless of the BWM/control axis.

**Site-A Asn enrichment in BWM at day 10 is the file's largest BWM-specific magnitude shift (exploratory).** BWM,A,N at `log2_OR`=+0.83 is the largest `|log2_OR|` in the BWM column; it also carries the file's largest `effect change` (+1.051) and sits alone at the top of the *significant in BWM only* section, with control,A,N essentially flat (`log2_OR`=-0.22, p_adj=0.053; not significant). On its face this would read as a BWM-specific gain of Asn at the A site by day 10, but the control,A panel is so heavily populated (9 FDR<0.05 hits: Q/L/W/S/Y depleted and G/R/K/I enriched) that the control side is *not* a flat reference — it is reshuffling in a different direction. So "BWM-specific Asn enrichment" is a candidate hypothesis rather than a finding: it could equally be (a) a real BWM-driven gain of Asn-at-A by day 10, or (b) a passive consequence of control losing Asn-at-A through composition redistribution that BWM did not undergo. Cross-checking against the codon-resolution sister CSV (does AAT or AAC carry the BWM,A,N signal at codon resolution?) and against the d10_vs_d5 aa contrast (does the Asn enrichment appear in the second half of the timecourse?) is the natural next step.

**The day-10-vs-day-0 difference between BWM and control hit counts (12 vs 24) is partly a power story (exploratory).** The 60-vs-60 test-count split is balanced, but if BWM and control have different total k counts in the underlying contingencies, the same effect size would produce different p magnitudes. The headline does not give the per-condition pooled totals for this file, so the 12-vs-24 gap is **not** decomposable here into "effect-size difference" vs "power difference" from the summary numbers alone. Treat the asymmetry as suggestive but not as evidence that "control reshuffles more than BWM"; carrying this question to the codon-resolution sister CSV and to the synthesis layer is the appropriate next step.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the broad control,P composition reshuffling (G/E/R/D/C up vs M/Q/K/I/L/W down), the E-site Lys direction flip between BWM and control, and the shared-direction P:E enriched and P:K depleted across both conditions as design-clean "within-condition timecourse drift" signals. *Exploratory*: the BWM-specific A:N enrichment (needs codon and d10_vs_d5 corroboration) and the BWM-vs-control hit-count asymmetry (needs per-condition pooled totals to resolve). *Noise*: low-count flagged rows (BWM,E,C; control,A,W; control,P,M; control,P,C; control,P,W; control,E,Y; control,E,M; control,E,W) — the `log2_OR` magnitudes are unstable at low day_10_count and large `|log2_OR|` may reflect sampling rather than biology.

## Methods
Fisher's exact (two-sided) is applied to a 2x2 of (feature_count, total - feature_count) at each timepoint within one condition. The script (`stall_sites_non_consensus_stats.py`) computes BH-FDR within each (condition, site) family of 20 tests for AA-level (so each of the 6 sub-families is corrected independently, not against the full 120). The test does **not** answer whether the same feature is changing in BWM and control in the same direction — that comparison is made by reading paired panels side-by-side (see "control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction" caveat) and is the largest-magnitude reading from this file's cells. Counts (`day_X_count`) and totals (`day_X_total`) are summed across replicates before the test, hence the family-level pseudoreplication caveat.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** 2x2 contingencies pool replicates by design; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so p-values are anti-conservative — read `log2_OR` as the primary effect column and treat the smallest p-magnitudes as inflated, not as evidence of additional certainty.
- **large-N-Fisher-anticonservative.** Pooled (day_10 + day_0) totals at the condition level are in the thousands (6091, 6945, 8788, 27732 across cells); even tiny relative deviations can drive `p_adj` to 1e-7 or below — rank by `log2_OR` when ordering effects within a (condition, site) family.
- **bh-per-(condition,site).** BH is run independently within each of the 6 (condition, site) families of 20 AAs; two cells with the same raw p in different (condition, site) families can land at different `p_adj` — rank within a (condition, site) cell, not across the full 120-test grid.
- **within-condition-clean.** No condition pooling and no timepoint pooling; the file structurally answers "does day_10 differ from day_0 *within* this condition" cleanly, leaving the BWM-vs-control comparison to be done by reading paired panels side-by-side rather than by the test itself.
- **rare-aa-low-count.** Rare AAs (W, M, C and several others by cell) carry `day_X_count` below 100 in BWM and below 200 in control across some (condition, site) cells (e.g. BWM,E,C with day_10=69, day_0=34). Rank by `log2_OR` but flag magnitude as unstable for these rows.
- **OR-direction-asymmetry.** No zero-cell rows in this file, so no infinite/zero ORs; the caveat is preserved as a ranking-axis discipline note — `log2_OR` is the reported effect column (already log-transformed), so positive/negative bands are symmetric by construction; rank directly on it.
- **control-vs-BWM-divergent-direction.** Three site x aa cells show BWM and control going opposite ways or one flat while the other moves: site-A N (BWM enriched `log2_OR`=+0.83 vs control `log2_OR`=-0.22 ns), site-P G (BWM `log2_OR`=+0.01 ns vs control enriched `log2_OR`=+0.36), site-E K (BWM depleted `log2_OR`=-0.34 vs control enriched `log2_OR`=+0.18, opposite directions). These are the design's *target* observations and should anchor the BWM-specific reading at the synthesis layer.

---

## Plots — individual

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM A-site amino-acid volcano: log2(OR) on x, -log10(FDR) on y; triangles are FDR<0.05. Asparagine (N, polar purple) at log2(OR) ~= +0.83 sits as the far-right outlier and highest single point in the panel; leucine (L, hydrophobic green), tyrosine (Y, hydrophobic green), and alanine (A, hydrophobic green) are FDR<0.05 depleted at log2(OR) ~= -0.35 to -0.45; isoleucine (I, green) and phenylalanine (F, green) are modestly enriched. All FDR<0.05 hits except N sit within the +/-0.5 log2(OR) band.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/BWM_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM P-site amino-acid volcano: only 2 FDR<0.05 hits — lysine (K, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= -0.40 (depleted) and glutamate (E, acidic red) at log2(OR) ~= +0.40 (enriched). All other amino acids collapse to a tight cluster near the origin; this is structurally the sparsest BWM panel of the row.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/BWM_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 BWM E-site amino-acid volcano: lysine (K, basic blue) at log2(OR) ~= -0.34 sits highest on the y-axis as the single depleted FDR<0.05 hit; on the positive side, glutamine (Q, polar purple), serine (S, polar purple), and cysteine (C, polar purple) are FDR<0.05 enriched at log2(OR) ~= +0.5 to +0.85, with C farthest right.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/BWM_E_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control A-site amino-acid volcano: 9 FDR<0.05 hits clustered across the +/-0.5 log2(OR) band — glutamine (Q, polar purple) and glycine (G, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= -0.6 and +0.36 sit at the top of the y-axis (-log10(FDR) ~= 7); leucine (L, green) and arginine (R, basic blue) are FDR<0.05 modestly displaced (~-0.34 and +0.34); tryptophan (W, green) is at log2(OR) ~= -0.6; lysine (K, blue), serine (S, polar), tyrosine (Y, green), and isoleucine (I, green) are also FDR<0.05 with smaller magnitudes.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/control_A_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control P-site amino-acid volcano: the most-populated panel of the file — 11 FDR<0.05 hits. Glycine (G, neutral orange) at log2(OR) ~= +0.36 sits highest (-log10(FDR) ~= 7.2); methionine (M, hydrophobic green) at log2(OR) ~= -0.68 and glutamine (Q, polar purple) at ~= -0.45 are the strongest depletions; lysine (K, basic blue), isoleucine (I, green), leucine (L, green), and tryptophan (W, green) are FDR<0.05 depleted within the +/-0.5 log2(OR) band; glutamate (E, acidic red), arginine (R, basic blue), aspartate (D, red), and cysteine (C, polar purple) are FDR<0.05 enriched.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/control_P_volcano.png)

![Day-10-vs-day-0 control E-site amino-acid volcano: a near-null panel — 4 FDR<0.05 hits, all within the +/-0.5 log2(OR) band. Tyrosine (Y, hydrophobic green) and methionine (M, green) are depleted (log2(OR) ~= -0.45 and -0.40); tryptophan (W, green) sits at the FDR threshold at log2(OR) ~= -0.55 (low-count flagged in Top hits); lysine (K, basic blue) is the lone FDR<0.05 enriched hit at log2(OR) ~= +0.18.](../../plots/within_condition_timepoint_fisher/d10_vs_d0/individual/control_E_volcano.png)
